query:
-- Clear existing meetings for all 4 deals
DELETE FROM meetings WHERE dealId IN (1, 2, 4, 60001) AND tenantId = 1;

-- ACME CORP meetings (6 meetings)
INSERT INTO meetings (dealId, tenantId, date, type, keyParticipant, summary, duration) VALUES
(1, 1, '2026-01-08 10:00:00', 'Discovery Call', 'Jennifer Walsh', 
'Leo opened by asking Jennifer to describe the biggest friction points in her team''s deal management process. Jennifer was candid: "We lost two $400K deals last quarter and I still don''t know exactly why. My AEs are managing 15-20 stakeholders per deal in Salesforce, but Salesforce just tracks activities — it doesn''t tell me who''s actually influential, who''s gone cold, or what changed in the last two weeks." She described a specific deal where their champion left the company mid-cycle and the AE had no backup relationship. Leo walked through Meridian''s stakeholder heat map and Jennifer immediately said "this is exactly what I''ve been trying to build manually in spreadsheets." She asked about integration with Salesforce and Gong. Leo confirmed native Salesforce sync and Gong transcript import. Jennifer committed to scheduling a demo with her RevOps director Rachel Torres. Key metric discussed: Jennifer''s team has a 23% win rate on deals over $200K — she wants to get to 35% by Q3. Budget was not discussed but Jennifer mentioned she has discretionary budget up to $150K and would need CTO approval above that.',
45),

(1, 1, '2026-01-22 14:00:00', 'Demo', 'Jennifer Walsh, Rachel Torres',
'Full product demo with Jennifer and Rachel Torres (RevOps Director). Leo demonstrated the stakeholder map using a sample deal with 8 stakeholders. Rachel was immediately engaged — she started asking technical questions about data model and API access. Jennifer focused on the AI deal insights, asking Leo to show how Meridian analyzes a deal with multiple competing stakeholders. Rachel asked about Salesforce field mapping and whether custom objects are supported. Leo confirmed custom object support is on the Q2 roadmap. Rachel flagged this as a potential issue since Acme uses 14 custom Salesforce objects. Jennifer asked about the transcript upload workflow — she wants AEs to be able to upload Gong recordings automatically. Leo demonstrated the manual upload flow and mentioned the Gong API integration is in beta. Rachel sent a follow-up email requesting a detailed technical requirements document. Jennifer asked Leo to schedule a meeting with David Park (CTO) — she said "David will have questions about security and data residency, and I want you to address those directly rather than through me." Positive meeting overall. Rachel rated the demo 8/10 in her follow-up email.',
75),

(1, 1, '2026-02-05 09:00:00', 'Technical Review', 'David Park, Rachel Torres',
'Technical review with CTO David Park and Rachel Torres. David opened with: "I want to understand exactly where our customer conversation data goes and who has access to it." Leo walked through the data architecture: all transcript data is encrypted at rest and in transit, processed in US-East AWS region, with no data sharing with third parties. David asked about SOC2 compliance — Leo confirmed SOC2 Type II report is available under NDA. David then asked about the AI model: "Are you using OpenAI? Because we have a policy against sending customer data to OpenAI." Leo explained that Meridian uses a private deployment with data processing agreements in place. David seemed satisfied but said he needed to loop in Michael Stern (CISO) for final approval. Rachel asked about the Salesforce integration architecture — specifically whether Meridian reads from or writes to Salesforce. Leo explained the bidirectional sync model. Rachel flagged that their Salesforce environment has strict field-level security and she would need to test the integration in their sandbox. David committed to a 2-week security review timeline. He mentioned the CISO review could add another 2-4 weeks. Rachel asked for a sandbox environment to test the Salesforce integration.',
60),

(1, 1, '2026-02-19 11:00:00', 'Technical Review', 'Rachel Torres',
'Follow-up technical session with Rachel Torres to walk through the Salesforce sandbox integration. Rachel had prepared a detailed test plan with 23 test cases covering field mapping, custom object sync, and data refresh frequency. Leo and Rachel worked through 18 of the 23 test cases — all passed. The 5 remaining cases involve custom object support which is not yet available. Rachel noted: "The core functionality works well. My main concern is the 5 custom object gaps — we use those objects for our enterprise account hierarchy, which is critical for how we structure deals." Leo committed to getting engineering to assess the custom object timeline. Rachel mentioned she had not heard from Michael Stern (CISO) and was not sure if he had started the security review. She offered to follow up with him directly. Rachel''s overall assessment: "If the custom object issue gets resolved and security clears, I''m ready to recommend this to Jennifer." She asked about implementation timeline and training resources.',
90),

(1, 1, '2026-03-04 15:00:00', 'Follow-up', 'Jennifer Walsh',
'Check-in call with Jennifer Walsh. Jennifer reported that the security review with Michael Stern has stalled — he sent the security questionnaire back with 12 questions but has not responded to Leo''s follow-up emails in 2 weeks. Jennifer said: "Michael is like this with every vendor. He''s not trying to kill the deal — he''s just slow. I''d recommend having your legal team send the DPA directly to our legal team rather than going through Michael." Jennifer also mentioned that David Park asked her about the custom object timeline. She said: "David is not going to block this deal over custom objects, but he wants to know it''s on the roadmap." Jennifer confirmed her budget is approved — she got verbal sign-off from David for $240K. She wants to close by end of Q1 but acknowledged the CISO review is the critical path. Leo agreed to have legal send the DPA directly and to provide a written commitment on the custom object roadmap.',
30),

(1, 1, '2026-03-18 10:00:00', 'Technical Review', 'Michael Stern, Rachel Torres',
'Long-awaited security review with CISO Michael Stern and Rachel Torres. Michael opened by saying he had reviewed the SOC2 report and had 4 remaining concerns: (1) data retention policy — he wants a 90-day maximum retention for transcript data, (2) right to deletion — he wants contractual guarantee of data deletion within 30 days of contract termination, (3) sub-processor list — he wants to approve any changes to the sub-processor list, (4) penetration test results — he wants to see the most recent pen test report. Leo addressed all 4 points: (1) 90-day retention is configurable, (2) deletion guarantee is standard in the DPA, (3) sub-processor change notification is in the DPA, (4) pen test report is available under NDA. Michael said: "These are reasonable answers. I''ll review the DPA this week and come back with any final questions." Rachel added: "Michael, if you can turn this around by end of week, we can still close this quarter." Michael agreed to a 5-day review timeline. This was a significant breakthrough — Michael has historically been the deal killer and he seems genuinely satisfied with the security posture.',
60);

-- GLOBALTECH meetings (5 meetings)
INSERT INTO meetings (dealId, tenantId, date, type, keyParticipant, summary, duration) VALUES
(2, 1, '2025-11-12 14:00:00', 'Discovery Call', 'Marcus Chen',
'First call with Marcus Chen, VP of Sales at GlobalTech. Marcus reached out after seeing Meridian mentioned in a Sales Hacker article. He described his problem clearly: "I have 45 AEs managing complex financial services deals. The average deal has 7 stakeholders and takes 8 months to close. I have no visibility into what''s happening at the stakeholder level — I only find out a deal is in trouble when it slips the forecast." Marcus asked about Meridian''s approach to deal intelligence vs. forecasting tools like Clari. Leo explained that Meridian focuses on the qualitative intelligence layer — stakeholder relationships, political dynamics, engagement trends — rather than pipeline math. Marcus said: "That''s exactly the gap. Clari tells me a deal is at risk but not why." He asked about pricing. Leo gave a range of $150K-$200K for GlobalTech''s team size. Marcus said: "That''s within my budget. Let''s move fast — I want to show something to my board in Q1." He asked to schedule a demo within the next 2 weeks.',
40),

(2, 1, '2025-11-26 10:00:00', 'Demo', 'Marcus Chen, Kevin O''Brien',
'Product demo with Marcus Chen and Kevin O''Brien (Head of RevOps). Kevin came prepared with a list of integration questions. Marcus focused on the AI deal insights — he asked Leo to simulate what Meridian would show for a deal that had gone quiet after a strong demo. Leo walked through the engagement heat map showing a stakeholder who had gone from High to Low engagement, and the AI insight flagging this as a risk. Marcus said: "This is exactly what I need. My AEs don''t notice these signals until it''s too late." Kevin asked about the Salesforce integration and whether Meridian could pull in Gong call data automatically. Leo confirmed both. Kevin then asked about the data model — specifically whether Meridian stores conversation content or just metadata. Leo explained the full transcript storage model with encryption. Kevin said he would need to review the data processing agreement but did not flag any blockers. Marcus asked about implementation timeline. Leo said 2-3 weeks for full deployment. Marcus said: "I want to start a pilot with 10 AEs in January. If it works, we expand to the full team in Q2." He asked Kevin to prepare an implementation plan.',
70),

(2, 1, '2026-01-08 09:00:00', 'Technical Review', 'Kevin O''Brien',
'Implementation planning session with Kevin O''Brien. Kevin had prepared a detailed Salesforce integration plan. He walked Leo through GlobalTech''s Salesforce configuration: standard objects only (no custom objects), Gong integration already in place, 45 AEs with standardized deal stages. Kevin said: "This is actually a clean implementation — your standard Salesforce connector should work out of the box." He asked about the pilot setup: 10 AEs, starting with their financial services vertical team. Kevin wanted to understand the onboarding workflow — specifically how AEs would start using Meridian for existing deals vs. new deals. Leo walked through the deal import flow. Kevin identified one issue: GlobalTech uses a non-standard deal stage naming convention ("Qualified" instead of "Discovery", "Proof of Value" instead of "POC"). Leo confirmed these are configurable. Kevin said: "I''m ready to start the pilot as soon as the contract is signed. I''ve already identified the 10 AEs and briefed them." He mentioned that Marcus had gotten verbal CFO approval for the budget. The main remaining step is contract negotiation.',
60),

(2, 1, '2026-02-14 14:00:00', 'Follow-up', 'Marcus Chen, Sandra Liu',
'Contract negotiation call with Marcus Chen and Sandra Liu (Procurement). Sandra opened by saying GlobalTech''s standard payment terms are net-60 and she was requesting a 20% discount from the quoted price of $180K. Leo explained that Meridian''s standard terms are net-30 and the quoted price already reflects a 15% volume discount. Sandra pushed back: "Every vendor we work with accepts net-60. This is non-negotiable." Marcus intervened: "Sandra, let''s focus on getting the deal done. Leo, what can you do on the commercial terms?" Leo offered a counter: net-45 payment terms and a 5% additional discount in exchange for a 2-year commitment (vs. 1-year). Marcus said: "I like the 2-year option — it gives us pricing stability." Sandra said she would need to review the 2-year terms with her manager. Marcus asked Leo to send a revised proposal with the 2-year option. After Sandra dropped off the call, Marcus said: "Don''t worry about Sandra — she does this with every vendor. The deal is done. I just need to give her something to show her manager." He confirmed the CFO has approved the budget and the main remaining step is legal review of the MSA.',
45),

(2, 1, '2026-03-10 11:00:00', 'Negotiation', 'Marcus Chen, Kevin O''Brien',
'Final pre-close call with Marcus and Kevin. Marcus confirmed that Sandra''s manager approved the 2-year terms with net-45 payment. Legal has reviewed the MSA and has 3 redlines: (1) limitation of liability cap — they want 2x ACV vs. Meridian''s standard 1x ACV, (2) data breach notification — they want 24-hour notification vs. Meridian''s standard 72-hour, (3) audit rights — they want annual audit rights for data processing. Kevin said: "These are standard enterprise asks. I don''t think any of them are deal-breakers." Marcus said: "I want to close this week. My Q1 board presentation is March 28th and I want to announce this as a Q1 win." Leo agreed to have legal review the redlines and respond within 48 hours. Kevin asked about the implementation kickoff timeline — he wants to start the pilot the week after signing. Leo committed to a kickoff call within 3 business days of contract execution. Marcus said: "This has been a great process. You''ve been responsive and the product does what you said it would. Let''s get this done."',
45);

-- NEXUS SYSTEMS meetings (5 meetings)
INSERT INTO meetings (dealId, tenantId, date, type, keyParticipant, summary, duration) VALUES
(4, 1, '2026-01-15 10:00:00', 'Discovery Call', 'Amanda Foster',
'First call with Amanda Foster, Director of RevOps at Nexus Systems. Amanda found Meridian through a LinkedIn post by Kevin O''Brien (GlobalTech) who mentioned the product. Amanda described the situation: "We have 200 AEs managing complex manufacturing deals. Our average deal involves 6-8 stakeholders and takes 10-12 months. We use Salesforce but our AEs treat it as a reporting tool, not a selling tool. I''m trying to build a business case for modernizing our sales tech stack." Amanda asked about Meridian''s approach to change management — she mentioned a failed Salesforce upgrade that had 40% adoption after 6 months. Leo walked through Meridian''s onboarding approach: structured pilot with 10-15 AEs, weekly check-ins, adoption metrics dashboard. Amanda said: "That''s more structured than what we did with Salesforce." She asked about pricing for a 200-person team. Leo gave a range of $300K-$400K. Amanda said: "That''s above my budget authority — I''d need CRO approval for anything over $100K. But let me build the business case first." She asked to schedule a demo with her and potentially Derek Santos (a top AE she wanted to include).',
45),

(4, 1, '2026-01-29 14:00:00', 'Demo', 'Amanda Foster, Derek Santos',
'Product demo with Amanda Foster and Derek Santos (Senior AE). Derek was immediately engaged — he started asking questions before Leo finished the introduction. Derek said: "I currently track my deal stakeholders in a Notion workspace because Salesforce is useless for this. Does Meridian replace that?" Leo walked through the stakeholder map and Derek said: "This is exactly what I''ve been trying to build manually." He asked about mobile access — he manages deals while traveling and needs to update stakeholder notes on the go. Leo showed the mobile-responsive web app. Derek asked: "Is there a native iOS app?" Leo said the native app is on the roadmap for Q3. Derek said: "The web app works for now." Amanda focused on the admin features — she asked about bulk import of existing deals from Salesforce, user management, and reporting. Leo walked through all three. Amanda asked about the AI deal insights — she wanted to understand how the AI generates recommendations. Leo demonstrated the transcript upload and AI analysis workflow. Amanda said: "This is the feature that will sell this to our CRO — he''s been asking for data-driven deal coaching for 2 years." Derek said: "I want to be a pilot user. When can we start?" Amanda said she needed to get CRO buy-in first but would move fast.',
80),

(4, 1, '2026-02-12 09:00:00', 'Follow-up', 'Amanda Foster',
'Business case review call with Amanda Foster. Amanda had prepared a 12-slide business case for CRO Robert Kim. She walked Leo through the deck and asked for help with the ROI section. The deck included: current state analysis (200 AEs, 23% win rate on enterprise deals, $2.1M average deal value), proposed solution (Meridian for 50 strategic AEs initially), projected impact (5% win rate improvement = $10.5M additional revenue annually). Amanda said: "The ROI math is compelling but I need to validate the 5% win rate assumption. Do you have customer data I can reference?" Leo shared two customer case studies: Acme Corp (projected 8% win rate improvement) and a financial services firm (achieved 12% win rate improvement in first year). Amanda incorporated these into her deck. She mentioned that Robert Kim has a board presentation on March 15th and she wants to present the Meridian business case to him before that. She said: "If I can get Robert''s buy-in before the board presentation, he might even mention it as a Q2 initiative." She asked Leo to be available for a call with Robert on short notice.',
60),

(4, 1, '2026-02-26 14:00:00', 'Technical Review', 'James Whitfield, Amanda Foster',
'Security review with James Whitfield (IT Security Director) and Amanda Foster. James had reviewed the SOC2 report Leo sent and came with a focused list of questions. James asked about: (1) data processing location — confirmed US-East AWS, (2) sub-processor list — Leo provided the full list, (3) penetration testing — Leo provided the most recent pen test summary, (4) data retention — Leo confirmed configurable retention up to 90 days. James said: "Your security posture is solid. I don''t have any blocking concerns." He asked for the full DPA to review with legal. Amanda said: "James, that''s great news. How long will legal review take?" James said: "Typically 2-3 weeks for a standard DPA." Amanda said: "Can we expedite? I''m trying to get CRO approval before the board presentation." James said he would flag it as priority. Amanda asked Leo to send the DPA directly to James and copy legal. James mentioned he would also need to review the Salesforce integration architecture — he wanted to confirm that Meridian only reads from Salesforce and does not write customer data back. Leo clarified the bidirectional sync model — Meridian writes deal stage updates and activity logs back to Salesforce. James said: "I need to review that more carefully — writing back to Salesforce is a higher risk profile than read-only."',
60),

(4, 1, '2026-03-12 10:00:00', 'Executive Briefing', 'Robert Kim, Amanda Foster, Lisa Nguyen',
'First meeting with CRO Robert Kim, joined by Amanda Foster and VP Sales Lisa Nguyen. Amanda had briefed Robert before the call. Robert opened with: "Amanda has been telling me about Meridian for weeks. I want to understand two things: how it will change how my AEs sell, and what the implementation risk is." Leo walked through the value proposition: Meridian gives AEs and managers real-time visibility into deal health at the stakeholder level, grounded in actual meeting evidence rather than gut feel. Robert said: "I like the concept. My concern is adoption — we rolled out a new Salesforce configuration last year and 60% of my AEs still aren''t using it correctly." Leo walked through the adoption approach: structured pilot, weekly coaching sessions, adoption dashboard. Lisa said: "The adoption concern is real. My AEs are already complaining about too many tools." Leo asked Lisa: "What would make them actually use it?" Lisa said: "If it saves them time on deal updates and gives them something useful before every meeting, they''ll use it." Leo demonstrated the pre-meeting brief feature. Lisa said: "That''s actually useful. Derek told me about this — he said it''s like having a research assistant." Robert asked about pricing. Leo said $320K for 50 AEs annually. Robert said: "That''s a significant investment. I need to see a pilot succeed before I commit to that." He asked for a proposal for a 10-AE pilot at $50K, with an option to expand. Amanda said: "Robert, the pilot option is exactly what I recommended." Robert said: "Get me the pilot proposal and let''s move forward."',
60);

-- NIKE meetings (4 meetings)
INSERT INTO meetings (dealId, tenantId, date, type, keyParticipant, summary, duration) VALUES
(60001, 1, '2026-03-05 09:00:00', 'Discovery Call', 'Sarah Kim',
'First call with Sarah Kim, VP of Sales Operations at Nike. Sarah reached out via LinkedIn after seeing Meridian mentioned at SaaStr Annual. Sarah described Nike''s B2B wholesale sales operation: 50 AEs managing relationships with major retailers (Foot Locker, Dick''s Sporting Goods, Nordstrom, etc.). Each AE manages 5-8 major accounts with complex buying committees. Sarah said: "Our AEs are managing relationships with 10-15 stakeholders per account. They track this in Salesforce, spreadsheets, and personal notes — it''s completely fragmented." She described a specific pain point: "We lost the Foot Locker account renewal last year because our champion left and the new buyer had no relationship with us. We didn''t even know the champion had left until 3 weeks after it happened." Leo walked through Meridian''s stakeholder tracking and engagement monitoring. Sarah said: "The engagement heat map is exactly what we need — we would have caught the Foot Locker situation immediately." She asked about pricing for 50 AEs. Leo said $400K-$500K annually. Sarah said: "That''s above my budget authority — I can approve up to $100K. I''d need VP Sales sign-off for the full deal." She asked about a pilot option. Leo said a 10-AE pilot at $80K is available. Sarah said: "That''s within my budget. Let me talk to James Rodriguez (VP Sales) about the full deal, but I can start the pilot conversation now." She asked to schedule a demo with Emily Zhao (Sales Enablement Director) who manages the tech stack.',
50),

(60001, 1, '2026-03-12 14:00:00', 'Demo', 'Sarah Kim, Emily Zhao',
'Product demo with Sarah Kim and Emily Zhao, Director of Sales Enablement. Emily came prepared with a competitive analysis she had done on Meridian, Gong, and Clari. Emily said: "I''ve been evaluating sales tools for 3 years. Gong is strong on call recording but weak on deal intelligence. Clari is strong on forecasting but doesn''t help AEs in the moment. Meridian seems to be filling a different gap." Leo confirmed: "We''re not trying to replace Gong or Clari — we''re the deal intelligence layer that sits on top of your existing stack." Emily said: "That''s a good positioning. Our AEs already use Gong and they won''t give it up." Leo demonstrated the Gong integration — transcripts from Gong automatically sync to Meridian and are analyzed for stakeholder signals. Emily said: "That''s the integration I was hoping for." She asked about Salesforce integration. Leo walked through the bidirectional sync. Emily asked about the Knowledge Base feature — she said: "We have a massive sales playbook that no one reads. If Meridian can make it searchable and contextual, that''s a huge win." Leo demonstrated the Knowledge Base and AI-grounded deal insights. Emily said: "This is the feature that differentiates you from everyone else." Sarah asked about implementation timeline. Leo said 3-4 weeks for full deployment. Sarah said: "I want to start the pilot in April. I''ll talk to James Rodriguez this week about the full deal."',
75),

(60001, 1, '2026-03-19 10:00:00', 'Technical Review', 'Emily Zhao, Brian Torres',
'Technical review with Emily Zhao and Brian Torres (IT Architecture Lead). Brian opened with: "Nike has strict data governance requirements. I need to understand exactly where our data goes and what controls we have." Leo walked through the data architecture in detail: AWS US-East primary, EU-West secondary for GDPR compliance, no data sharing with third parties, customer data isolated per tenant. Brian asked about data residency options — specifically whether Nike could require all data to stay in US regions. Leo confirmed US-only data residency is available as a configuration option. Brian asked about the AI processing: "When you run AI analysis on our transcripts, does that data go to OpenAI?" Leo explained the private deployment model with data processing agreements. Brian said: "I need to see the sub-processor list and the DPA." Leo committed to sending both within 24 hours. Emily asked about the Salesforce integration architecture — specifically whether Meridian supports Salesforce Shield (Nike''s enterprise security layer). Leo said Salesforce Shield compatibility is in the Q2 roadmap. Emily flagged this as a potential issue: "We require Salesforce Shield compatibility for any tool that integrates with our Salesforce instance." Brian said: "That''s a hard requirement for us." Leo said he would check with engineering on the timeline. Emily said: "If you can commit to Salesforce Shield by Q2, we can work around it for the pilot." Brian said: "I''m satisfied with the security posture overall — the Shield issue is the main technical gap."',
60),

(60001, 1, '2026-03-24 15:00:00', 'Follow-up', 'Sarah Kim',
'Check-in call with Sarah Kim. Sarah reported that James Rodriguez (VP Sales) is interested but has competing priorities — a major SAP implementation is consuming his attention. Sarah said: "James told me to move forward with the pilot evaluation and present him with results in 6 weeks. He''s not blocking the deal, he just can''t give it attention right now." Sarah asked about the Salesforce Shield issue raised by Brian Torres. Leo confirmed engineering has assessed the timeline: Salesforce Shield compatibility will be available in the June release. Sarah said: "June works for us — the pilot would be running April-May and we wouldn''t need Shield until full deployment." She asked about the pilot structure: 10 AEs, starting with Michelle Park''s team (strategic accounts). Sarah said: "Michelle is excited to start. She told me she''s been manually building a stakeholder map in Notion for every account — Meridian would replace that completely." Sarah asked about pricing for the pilot. Leo confirmed $80K for the 10-AE pilot with a credit toward the full deployment. Sarah said: "I can approve that. Let me get the paperwork started." She asked Leo to send the pilot agreement by end of week.',
35);

(1, 1, '2026-03-18 10:00:00', 'Technical Review', 'Michael Stern, Rachel Torres',
'Long-awaited security review with CISO Michael Stern and Rachel Torres. Michael opened by saying he had reviewed the SOC2 report and had 4 remaining concerns: (1) data retention policy — he wants a 90-day maximum retention for transcript data, (2) right to deletion — he wants contractual guarantee of data deletion within 30 days of contract termination, (3) sub-processor list — he wants to approve any changes to the sub-processor list, (4) penetration test results — he wants to see the most recent pen test report. Leo addressed all 4 points: (1) 90-day retention is configurable, (2) deletion guarantee is standard in the DPA, (3) sub-processor change notification is in the DPA, (4) pen test report is available under NDA. Michael said: "These are reasonable answers. I''ll review the DPA this week and come back with any final questions." Rachel added: "Michael, if you can turn this around by end of week, we can still close this quarter." Michael agreed to a 5-day review timeline. This was a significant breakthrough — Michael has historically been the deal killer and he seems genuinely satisfied with the security posture.',
60);

-- GLOBALTECH meetings (5 meetings)
INSERT INTO meetings (dealId, tenantId, date, type, keyParticipant, summary, duration) VALUES
(2, 1, '2025-11-12 14:00:00', 'Discovery Call', 'Marcus Chen',
'First call with Marcus Chen, VP of Sales at GlobalTech. Marcus reached out after seeing Meridian mentioned in a Sales Hacker article. He described his problem clearly: "I have 45 AEs managing complex financial services deals. The average deal has 7 stakeholders and takes 8 months to close. I have no visibility into what''s happening at the stakeholder level — I only find out a deal is in trouble when it slips the forecast." Marcus asked about Meridian''s approach to deal intelligence vs. forecasting tools like Clari. Leo explained that Meridian focuses on the qualitative intelligence layer — stakeholder relationships, political dynamics, engagement trends — rather than pipeline math. Marcus said: "That''s exactly the gap. Clari tells me a deal is at risk but not why." He asked about pricing. Leo gave a range of $150K-$200K for GlobalTech''s team size. Marcus said: "That''s within my budget. Let''s move fast — I want to show something to my board in Q1." He asked to schedule a demo within the next 2 weeks.',
40),

(2, 1, '2025-11-26 10:00:00', 'Demo', 'Marcus Chen, Kevin O''Brien',
'Product demo with Marcus Chen and Kevin O''Brien (Head of RevOps). Kevin came prepared with a list of integration questions. Marcus focused on the AI deal insights — he asked Leo to simulate what Meridian would show for a deal that had gone quiet after a strong demo. Leo walked through the engagement heat map showing a stakeholder who had gone from High to Low engagement, and the AI insight flagging this as a risk. Marcus said: "This is exactly what I need. My AEs don''t notice these signals until it''s too late." Kevin asked about the Salesforce integration and whether Meridian could pull in Gong call data automatically. Leo confirmed both. Kevin then asked about the data model — specifically whether Meridian stores conversation content or just metadata. Leo explained the full transcript storage model with encryption. Kevin said he would need to review the data processing agreement but did not flag any blockers. Marcus asked about implementation timeline. Leo said 2-3 weeks for full deployment. Marcus said: "I want to start a pilot with 10 AEs in January. If it works, we expand to the full team in Q2." He asked Kevin to prepare an implementation plan.',
70),

(2, 1, '2026-01-08 09:00:00', 'Technical Review', 'Kevin O''Brien',
'Implementation planning session with Kevin O''Brien. Kevin had prepared a detailed Salesforce integration plan. He walked Leo through GlobalTech''s Salesforce configuration: standard objects only (no custom objects), Gong integration already in place, 45 AEs with standardized deal stages. Kevin said: "This is actually a clean implementation — your standard Salesforce connector should work out of the box." He asked about the pilot setup: 10 AEs, starting with their financial services vertical team. Kevin wanted to understand the onboarding workflow — specifically how AEs would start using Meridian for existing deals vs. new deals. Leo walked through the deal import flow. Kevin identified one issue: GlobalTech uses a non-standard deal stage naming convention ("Qualified" instead of "Discovery", "Proof of Value" instead of "POC"). Leo confirmed these are configurable. Kevin said: "I''m ready to start the pilot as soon as the contract is signed. I''ve already identified the 10 AEs and briefed them." He mentioned that Marcus had gotten verbal CFO approval for the budget. The main remaining step is contract negotiation.',
60),

(2, 1, '2026-02-14 14:00:00', 'Follow-up', 'Marcus Chen, Sandra Liu',
'Contract negotiation call with Marcus Chen and Sandra Liu (Procurement). Sandra opened by saying GlobalTech''s standard payment terms are net-60 and she was requesting a 20% discount from the quoted price of $180K. Leo explained that Meridian''s standard terms are net-30 and the quoted price already reflects a 15% volume discount. Sandra pushed back: "Every vendor we work with accepts net-60. This is non-negotiable." Marcus intervened: "Sandra, let''s focus on getting the deal done. Leo, what can you do on the commercial terms?" Leo offered a counter: net-45 payment terms and a 5% additional discount in exchange for a 2-year commitment (vs. 1-year). Marcus said: "I like the 2-year option — it gives us pricing stability." Sandra said she would need to review the 2-year terms with her manager. Marcus asked Leo to send a revised proposal with the 2-year option. After Sandra dropped off the call, Marcus said: "Don''t worry about Sandra — she does this with every vendor. The deal is done. I just need to give her something to show her manager." He confirmed the CFO has approved the budget and the main remaining step is legal review of the MSA.',
45),

(2, 1, '2026-03-10 11:00:00', 'Negotiation', 'Marcus Chen, Kevin O''Brien',
'Final pre-close call with Marcus and Kevin. Marcus confirmed that Sandra''s manager approved the 2-year terms with net-45 payment. Legal has reviewed the MSA and has 3 redlines: (1) limitation of liability cap — they want 2x ACV vs. Meridian''s standard 1x ACV, (2) data breach notification — they want 24-hour notification vs. Meridian''s standard 72-hour, (3) audit rights — they want annual audit rights for data processing. Kevin said: "These are standard enterprise asks. I don''t think any of them are deal-breakers." Marcus said: "I want to close this week. My Q1 board presentation is March 28th and I want to announce this as a Q1 win." Leo agreed to have legal review the redlines and respond within 48 hours. Kevin asked about the implementation kickoff timeline — he wants to start the pilot the week after signing. Leo committed to a kickoff call within 3 business days of contract execution. Marcus said: "This has been a great process. You''ve been responsive and the product does what you said it would. Let''s get this done."',
45);

-- NEXUS SYSTEMS meetings (5 meetings)
INSERT INTO meetings (dealId, tenantId, date, type, keyParticipant, summary, duration) VALUES
(4, 1, '2026-01-15 10:00:00', 'Discovery Call', 'Amanda Foster',
'First call with Amanda Foster, Director of RevOps at Nexus Systems. Amanda found Meridian through a LinkedIn post by Kevin O''Brien (GlobalTech) who mentioned the product. Amanda described the situation: "We have 200 AEs managing complex manufacturing deals. Our average deal involves 6-8 stakeholders and takes 10-12 months. We use Salesforce but our AEs treat it as a reporting tool, not a selling tool. I''m trying to build a business case for modernizing our sales tech stack." Amanda asked about Meridian''s approach to change management — she mentioned a failed Salesforce upgrade that had 40% adoption after 6 months. Leo walked through Meridian''s onboarding approach: structured pilot with 10-15 AEs, weekly check-ins, adoption metrics dashboard. Amanda said: "That''s more structured than what we did with Salesforce." She asked about pricing for a 200-person team. Leo gave a range of $300K-$400K. Amanda said: "That''s above my budget authority — I''d need CRO approval for anything over $100K. But let me build the business case first." She asked to schedule a demo with her and potentially Derek Santos (a top AE she wanted to include).',
45),

(4, 1, '2026-01-29 14:00:00', 'Demo', 'Amanda Foster, Derek Santos',
'Product demo with Amanda Foster and Derek Santos (Senior AE). Derek was immediately engaged — he started asking questions before Leo finished the introduction. Derek said: "I currently track my deal stakeholders in a Notion workspace because Salesforce is useless for this. Does Meridian replace that?" Leo walked through the stakeholder map and Derek said: "This is exactly what I''ve been trying to build manually." He asked about mobile access — he manages deals while traveling and needs to update stakeholder notes on the go. Leo showed the mobile-responsive web app. Derek asked: "Is there a native iOS app?" Leo said the native app is on the roadmap for Q3. Derek said: "The web app works for now." Amanda focused on the admin features — she asked about bulk import of existing deals from Salesforce, user management, and reporting. Leo walked through all three. Amanda asked about the AI deal insights — she wanted to understand how the AI generates recommendations. Leo demonstrated the transcript upload and AI analysis workflow. Amanda said: "This is the feature that will sell this to our CRO — he''s been asking for data-driven deal coaching for 2 years." Derek said: "I want to be a pilot user. When can we start?" Amanda said she needed to get CRO buy-in first but would move fast.',
80),

(4, 1, '2026-02-12 09:00:00', 'Follow-up', 'Amanda Foster',
'Business case review call with Amanda Foster. Amanda had prepared a 12-slide business case for CRO Robert Kim. She walked Leo through the deck and asked for help with the ROI section. The deck included: current state analysis (200 AEs, 23% win rate on enterprise deals, $2.1M average deal value), proposed solution (Meridian for 50 strategic AEs initially), projected impact (5% win rate improvement = $10.5M additional revenue annually). Amanda said: "The ROI math is compelling but I need to validate the 5% win rate assumption. Do you have customer data I can reference?" Leo shared two customer case studies: Acme Corp (projected 8% win rate improvement) and a financial services firm (achieved 12% win rate improvement in first year). Amanda incorporated these into her deck. She mentioned that Robert Kim has a board presentation on March 15th and she wants to present the Meridian business case to him before that. She said: "If I can get Robert''s buy-in before the board presentation, he might even mention it as a Q2 initiative." She asked Leo to be available for a call with Robert on short notice.',
60),

(4, 1, '2026-02-26 14:00:00', 'Technical Review', 'James Whitfield, Amanda Foster',
'Security review with James Whitfield (IT Security Director) and Amanda Foster. James had reviewed the SOC2 report Leo sent and came with a focused list of questions. James asked about: (1) data processing location — confirmed US-East AWS, (2) sub-processor list — Leo provided the full list, (3) penetration testing — Leo provided the most recent pen test summary, (4) data retention — Leo confirmed configurable retention up to 90 days. James said: "Your security posture is solid. I don''t have any blocking concerns." He asked for the full DPA to review with legal. Amanda said: "James, that''s great news. How long will legal review take?" James said: "Typically 2-3 weeks for a standard DPA." Amanda said: "Can we expedite? I''m trying to get CRO approval before the board presentation." James said he would flag it as priority. Amanda asked Leo to send the DPA directly to James and copy legal. James mentioned he would also need to review the Salesforce integration architecture — he wanted to confirm that Meridian only reads from Salesforce and does not write customer data back. Leo clarified the bidirectional sync model — Meridian writes deal stage updates and activity logs back to Salesforce. James said: "I need to review that more carefully — writing back to Salesforce is a higher risk profile than read-only."',
60),

(4, 1, '2026-03-12 10:00:00', 'Executive Briefing', 'Robert Kim, Amanda Foster, Lisa Nguyen',
'First meeting with CRO Robert Kim, joined by Amanda Foster and VP Sales Lisa Nguyen. Amanda had briefed Robert before the call. Robert opened with: "Amanda has been telling me about Meridian for weeks. I want to understand two things: how it will change how my AEs sell, and what the implementation risk is." Leo walked through the value proposition: Meridian gives AEs and managers real-time visibility into deal health at the stakeholder level, grounded in actual meeting evidence rather than gut feel. Robert said: "I like the concept. My concern is adoption — we rolled out a new Salesforce configuration last year and 60% of my AEs still aren''t using it correctly." Leo walked through the adoption approach: structured pilot, weekly coaching sessions, adoption dashboard. Lisa said: "The adoption concern is real. My AEs are already complaining about too many tools." Leo asked Lisa: "What would make them actually use it?" Lisa said: "If it saves them time on deal updates and gives them something useful before every meeting, they''ll use it." Leo demonstrated the pre-meeting brief feature. Lisa said: "That''s actually useful. Derek told me about this — he said it''s like having a research assistant." Robert asked about pricing. Leo said $320K for 50 AEs annually. Robert said: "That''s a significant investment. I need to see a pilot succeed before I commit to that." He asked for a proposal for a 10-AE pilot at $50K, with an option to expand. Amanda said: "Robert, the pilot option is exactly what I recommended." Robert said: "Get me the pilot proposal and let''s move forward."',
60);

-- NIKE meetings (4 meetings)
INSERT INTO meetings (dealId, tenantId, date, type, keyParticipant, summary, duration) VALUES
(60001, 1, '2026-03-05 09:00:00', 'Discovery Call', 'Sarah Kim',
'First call with Sarah Kim, VP of Sales Operations at Nike. Sarah reached out via LinkedIn after seeing Meridian mentioned at SaaStr Annual. Sarah described Nike''s B2B wholesale sales operation: 50 AEs managing relationships with major retailers (Foot Locker, Dick''s Sporting Goods, Nordstrom, etc.). Each AE manages 5-8 major accounts with complex buying committees. Sarah said: "Our AEs are managing relationships with 10-15 stakeholders per account. They track this in Salesforce, spreadsheets, and personal notes — it''s completely fragmented." She described a specific pain point: "We lost the Foot Locker account renewal last year because our champion left and the new buyer had no relationship with us. We didn''t even know the champion had left until 3 weeks after it happened." Leo walked through Meridian''s stakeholder tracking and engagement monitoring. Sarah said: "The engagement heat map is exactly what we need — we would have caught the Foot Locker situation immediately." She asked about pricing for 50 AEs. Leo said $400K-$500K annually. Sarah said: "That''s above my budget authority — I can approve up to $100K. I''d need VP Sales sign-off for the full deal." She asked about a pilot option. Leo said a 10-AE pilot at $80K is available. Sarah said: "That''s within my budget. Let me talk to James Rodriguez (VP Sales) about the full deal, but I can start the pilot conversation now." She asked to schedule a demo with Emily Zhao (Sales Enablement Director) who manages the tech stack.',
50),

(60001, 1, '2026-03-12 14:00:00', 'Demo', 'Sarah Kim, Emily Zhao',
'Product demo with Sarah Kim and Emily Zhao, Director of Sales Enablement. Emily came prepared with a competitive analysis she had done on Meridian, Gong, and Clari. Emily said: "I''ve been evaluating sales tools for 3 years. Gong is strong on call recording but weak on deal intelligence. Clari is strong on forecasting but doesn''t help AEs in the moment. Meridian seems to be filling a different gap." Leo confirmed: "We''re not trying to replace Gong or Clari — we''re the deal intelligence layer that sits on top of your existing stack." Emily said: "That''s a good positioning. Our AEs already use Gong and they won''t give it up." Leo demonstrated the Gong integration — transcripts from Gong automatically sync to Meridian and are analyzed for stakeholder signals. Emily said: "That''s the integration I was hoping for." She asked about Salesforce integration. Leo walked through the bidirectional sync. Emily asked about the Knowledge Base feature — she said: "We have a massive sales playbook that no one reads. If Meridian can make it searchable and contextual, that''s a huge win." Leo demonstrated the Knowledge Base and AI-grounded deal insights. Emily said: "This is the feature that differentiates you from everyone else." Sarah asked about implementation timeline. Leo said 3-4 weeks for full deployment. Sarah said: "I want to start the pilot in April. I''ll talk to James Rodriguez this week about the full deal."',
75),

(60001, 1, '2026-03-19 10:00:00', 'Technical Review', 'Emily Zhao, Brian Torres',
'Technical review with Emily Zhao and Brian Torres (IT Architecture Lead). Brian opened with: "Nike has strict data governance requirements. I need to understand exactly where our data goes and what controls we have." Leo walked through the data architecture in detail: AWS US-East primary, EU-West secondary for GDPR compliance, no data sharing with third parties, customer data isolated per tenant. Brian asked about data residency options — specifically whether Nike could require all data to stay in US regions. Leo confirmed US-only data residency is available as a configuration option. Brian asked about the AI processing: "When you run AI analysis on our transcripts, does that data go to OpenAI?" Leo explained the private deployment model with data processing agreements. Brian said: "I need to see the sub-processor list and the DPA." Leo committed to sending both within 24 hours. Emily asked about the Salesforce integration architecture — specifically whether Meridian supports Salesforce Shield (Nike''s enterprise security layer). Leo said Salesforce Shield compatibility is in the Q2 roadmap. Emily flagged this as a potential issue: "We require Salesforce Shield compatibility for any tool that integrates with our Salesforce instance." Brian said: "That''s a hard requirement for us." Leo said he would check with engineering on the timeline. Emily said: "If you can commit to Salesforce Shield by Q2, we can work around it for the pilot." Brian said: "I''m satisfied with the security posture overall — the Shield issue is the main technical gap."',
60),

(60001, 1, '2026-03-24 15:00:00', 'Follow-up', 'Sarah Kim',
'Check-in call with Sarah Kim. Sarah reported that James Rodriguez (VP Sales) is interested but has competing priorities — a major SAP implementation is consuming his attention. Sarah said: "James told me to move forward with the pilot evaluation and present him with results in 6 weeks. He''s not blocking the deal, he just can''t give it attention right now." Sarah asked about the Salesforce Shield issue raised by Brian Torres. Leo confirmed engineering has assessed the timeline: Salesforce Shield compatibility will be available in the June release. Sarah said: "June works for us — the pilot would be running April-May and we wouldn''t need Shield until full deployment." She asked about the pilot structure: 10 AEs, starting with Michelle Park''s team (strategic accounts). Sarah said: "Michelle is excited to start. She told me she''s been manually building a stakeholder map in Notion for every account — Meridian would replace that completely." Sarah asked about pricing for the pilot. Leo confirmed $80K for the 10-AE pilot with a credit toward the full deployment. Sarah said: "I can approve that. Let me get the paperwork started." She asked Leo to send the pilot agreement by end of week.',
35);
